member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.Channel.Dispose
member.summary: Immediately terminates the channel and shuts down any ongoing communication.
member.remarks: Because this method may terminate the channel immediately and thus can cause previously queued content to not actually be received by the remote party, consider this method a "break glass" way of terminating a channel. The preferred method is that both sides "complete writing" and let the channel dispose itself.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.Channel.ProcessOutboundTransmissionsAsync
member.summary: Relays data that the local channel owner wants to send to the remote party.
member.remarks: This method takes ownership of and guarantees that it will be completed by the time this method completes, whether successfully or in a faulted state. To protect thread-safety, this method replaces the field with an instance of so no one else can mess with it and introduce thread-safety bugs. The field is restored as this method exits.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ChannelOptions.ExistingPipe
member.summary: Gets or sets an existing instance used to exchange data with the channel.

remarks.para: This property supports high throughput scenarios where channel data ultimately goes to a and that already exist. This may remove the need for a memory copy of all bytes transferred over the channel by directing the to read and write directly an existing reader/writer pair. When set, the and properties will throw since their values are implementation details of the existing pipe set here.
The specified in *must* be created with that *exceeds* the value of and .

member.exception: Thrown if set to an that returns for either of its properties.

member.summary: Gets or sets the number of received bytes that may be buffered locally per channel (transmitted from the remote party but not yet processed).
member.remarks: This value should be at least the value of when the was created. When the value is null or less than , the value from is used.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.Offer
member.summary: A channel is proposed to the remote party.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.OfferAccepted
member.summary: A channel proposal has been accepted.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.Content
member.summary: The payload of the frame is a payload intended for channel consumption.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.ContentWritingCompleted
member.summary: Sent after all bytes have been transmitted on a given channel. Either or both sides may send this. A channel may be automatically closed when each side has both transmitted and received this message.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.ChannelTerminated
member.summary: Sent when a channel is closed, an incoming offer is rejected, or an outgoing offer is canceled.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlCode.ContentProcessed
member.summary: Sent when a channel has finished processing data received from the remote party, allowing them to send more data.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlChannelId
member.summary: The channel id reserved for control frames.

member.summary: The maximum length of a frame's payload.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ControlFrameEncoding

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.DefaultChannelOptions
member.summary: The options to use for channels we create in response to incoming offers.
member.remarks: Whatever these settings are, they can be replaced when the channel is accepted.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.isOdd
member.summary: A value indicating whether this is the "odd" party in the conversation (where the other one would be "even").
member.remarks: This value is only significant for parts of the protocol where it's useful to have the two parties behave slightly differently to avoid conflicts.

member.name: E:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ChannelOffered
member.summary: Occurs when the remote party offers to establish a channel.
member.remarks: When adding a handler to this event, consider whether the stream has already started listening and you may have already missed some incoming events. If you want to receive *all* events from the start, be sure to create the with set to , then add handler(s) to this event before calling .

member.name: F:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.TraceEventId.ContentDiscardedOnDisposedChannel

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.StartListening
member.summary: Starts reading from the stream.
member.remarks: This should only be done once, on an instance created with set to and only after any required event handlers have been added so as to avoid a race where incoming messages lead to raising events before handlers have been added.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.CreateChannel(Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ChannelOptions)
member.summary: Creates an anonymous channel that may be accepted by . Its existance must be communicated by other means (typically another, existing channel) to encourage acceptance.
member.param: A set of options that describe local treatment of this channel.
member.returns: The anonymous channel.
member.remarks: Note that while the channel is created immediately, any local write to that channel will be buffered locally until the remote party accepts the channel.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.AcceptChannel(System.UInt64,Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ChannelOptions)
member.summary: Accepts a channel with a specific ID.
member.param: The of the channel to accept.
A set of options that describe local treatment of this channel.
member.returns: The accepted .
member.exception: Thrown if the channel is already accepted or is no longer offered by the remote party.
member.remarks: This method can be used to accept anonymous channels created with . Unlike which will await for a channel offer if a matching one has not been made yet, this method only accepts an offer for a channel that has already been made.
member.exception: Thrown when no channel with the specified exists.

member.exception: Thrown if the channel was already accepted.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.OfferChannelAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Offers a new, named channel to the remote party so they may accept it with .
member.param: A name for the channel, which must be accepted on the remote end to complete creation. It need not be unique, and may be empty but must not be null. Any characters are allowed, and max length is determined by the maximum frame payload (based on UTF-8 encoding).
A cancellation token.
member.returns: A task that completes with the if the offer is accepted on the remote end or faults with if the remote end rejects the channel.
member.exception: Thrown if is canceled before the channel is accepted by the remote end.

A cancellation token.
member.returns: A task that completes with the if the offer is accepted on the remote end or faults with if the remote end rejects the channel.
member.exception: Thrown if is canceled before the channel is accepted by the remote end.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.AcceptChannelAsync(System.String,Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.ChannelOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Accepts a channel that the remote end has attempted or may attempt to create.
member.param: The name of the channel to accept. An empty string will match an offer made via with an empty channel name. It will also match an anonymous channel offer made with .
A set of options that describe local treatment of this channel.
A token to indicate lost interest in accepting the channel.
member.returns: The , after its offer has been received from the remote party and accepted.
member.remarks: If multiple offers exist with the specified , the first one received will be accepted.
member.exception: Thrown if the channel is already accepted or is no longer offered by the remote party.
Thrown if is canceled before a request to create the channel has been received.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.Options.ProtocolMajorVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the protocol version to be used.
member.remarks: 1 is the original and default version. 2 is a protocol breaking change and adds backpressure support. 3 is a protocol breaking change that removes the initial handshake so no round-trip to establish the connection is necessary.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.Options.StartSuspended
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the should not start reading from the stream immediately in order to provide time for the creator to add event handlers such as .

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.MultiplexingStream.Options.SeededChannels
member.summary: Gets a list of options for channels that are to be "seeded" into a new .
member.remarks: Seeded channels avoid the need for a round-trip for an offer/accept packet exchange. Seeded channels are accessed within the instance by calling with the 0-based index into this list used as the channel ID. They are only supported when is at least 3.

Optional pipe options to use.
A cancellation token that aborts reading from the .

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.PipeExtensions.UsePipeWriter(System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocket,System.IO.Pipelines.PipeOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Enables efficiently writing to a using a .
member.param: The web socket to write to using a pipe.
Optional pipe options to use.
A cancellation token that aborts writing to the .
member.returns: A .
member.remarks: Because a has no concept of message boundaries, this API should only be used when the remote party can process a stream of bytes, ignoring where the message boundaries may happen to land. As implemented by this API, message boundaries may only appear at positions where or are called, but they are not guaranteed to be inserted for each of these calls.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.Streams.PipeExtensions.UseUtf8TextPipeWriter(System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocket,System.IO.Pipelines.PipeOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Enables efficiently writing UTF-8 encoded text to a using a .
member.param: The web socket to write to using a pipe.
Optional pipe options to use.
A cancellation token that aborts writing to the .
member.returns: A .

remarks.para: Although itself takes bytes, it is the caller's responsibility to only write bytes that represent UTF-8 encoded characters.

Because a has no concept of message boundaries, this API should only be used when the remote party can process a stream of bytes, ignoring where the message boundaries may happen to land. As implemented by this API, message boundaries may only appear at positions where or are called, but they are not guaranteed to be inserted for each of these calls.
Users should be sure to write whole UTF-8 characters to the . In particular, never call after writing only a subset of the bytes for any UTF-8 character.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.MinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum length for any array allocated as a segment in the sequence. Any non-positive value allows the pool to determine the length of the array.

remarks.para: Each time or is called, previously allocated memory is used if it is large enough to satisfy the length demand. If new memory must be allocated, the argument to one of these methods typically dictate the length of array to allocate. When the caller uses very small values (just enough for its immediate need) but the high level scenario can predict that a large amount of memory will be ultimately required, it can be advisable to set this property to a value such that just a few larger arrays are allocated instead of many small ones.
The in use may itself have a minimum array length as well, in which case the higher of the two minimums dictate the minimum array size that will be allocated.
If is , this value may be automatically increased as the length of a sequence grows.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.Streams.Sequence`1.AutoIncreaseMinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the should be intelligently increased as the length of the sequence grows.
member.remarks: This can help prevent long sequences made up of many very small arrays.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.Streams.UnownedPipeReader
member.summary: A substitute that throws for nearly every call, notifying the caller that they don't own this object. The only allowed call is to because that is assumed to be thread-safe.
member.remarks: This can be useful to set on a field that contains a while some async method "owns" it. One example is at disposal, the 'owning' object may be tempted to call on it, but this can cause thread-safety bugs and data corruption when the async reader loop is still using it. Instead, such a Dispose method should cancel the reader loop in a thread-safe fashion, and then either that read method or the Dispose method can Complete the reader.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: M:System.Index.GetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:MessagePack.SequencePool
member.summary: A thread-safe, alloc-free reusable object pool.

member.name: F:MessagePack.SequencePool.Shared
member.summary: A thread-safe pool of reusable objects.
member.remarks: We use a that allows every processor to be involved in messagepack serialization concurrently, plus one nested serialization per processor (since LZ4 and sometimes other nested serializations may exist).

member.name: F:MessagePack.SequencePool.MinimumSpanLength
member.summary: The value to use for .
member.remarks: Individual users that want a different value for this can modify the setting on the rented or by supplying their own .
member.devremarks: We use 32KB so that when LZ4Codec.MaximumOutputLength is used on this length it does not require a buffer that would require the Large Object Heap.

member.name: F:MessagePack.SequencePool.arrayPool
member.summary: The array pool which we share with all objects created by this instance.
member.devremarks: We allow 100 arrays to be shared (instead of the default 50) and reduce the max array length from the default 1MB to something more reasonable for our expected use.

member.name: M:MessagePack.SequencePool.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The maximum size to allow the pool to grow.

member.name: M:MessagePack.SequencePool.Rent
member.summary: Gets an instance of This is taken from the recycled pool if one is available; otherwise a new one is created.
member.returns: The rental tracker that provides access to the object as well as a means to return it.

member.name: P:MessagePack.SequencePool.Rental.Value
member.summary: Gets the recyclable object.

member.name: M:MessagePack.SequencePool.Rental.Dispose
member.summary: Returns the recyclable object to the pool.
member.remarks: The instance is cleaned first, if a clean delegate was provided. It is dropped instead of being returned to the pool if the pool is already at its maximum size.

member.name: M:MessagePack.MessagePackStreamReader.ReadAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Reads the next whole (top-level) messagepack data structure.
member.param: A cancellation token.
member.returns: A task whose result is the next whole data structure from the stream, or null if the stream ends. The returned sequence is valid until this is disposed or until this method is called again, whichever comes first.
member.remarks: When null is the result of the returned task, any extra bytes read (between the last complete message and the end of the stream) will be available via the property.

member.name: M:MessagePack.MessagePackStreamReader.DiscardBufferedData
member.summary: Arranges for the next read operation to start by reading from the underlying instead of any data buffered from a previous read.
member.remarks: This is appropriate if the underlying has been repositioned such that any previously buffered data is no longer applicable to what the caller wants to read.

member.name: M:MessagePack.MessagePackStreamReader.RecycleLastMessage
member.summary: Recycle memory from a previously returned message.

member.name: M:MessagePack.MessagePackStreamReader.TryReadMoreDataAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Read more data from the stream into the buffer.
member.param: A cancellation token.
member.returns: true if more data was read; false if the end of the stream had already been reached.

member.name: M:MessagePack.MessagePackStreamReader.TryReadNextMessage(System.Buffers.ReadOnlySequence{System.Byte}@)
member.summary: Checks whether the content in include a complete messagepack structure.
member.param: Receives the sequence of the first complete data structure found, if any.
member.returns: true if a complete data structure was found; false otherwise.
